{
  "risk": "HIGH",
  "riskReason": "Negative offset support bypasses pagination bounds without proper validation, enabling unauthorized data access and potential denial-of-service.",
  "circularTests": {

  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Security - Authorization Bypass",
      "file": "src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_auditlogs.py",
      "line": 74,
      "title": "Insufficient authorization check for optimized pagination feature",
      "description": "The authorization logic checks `request.user.is_superuser or organization_context.member.has_global_access`, but this is evaluated before instantiation. If `organization_context.member` is None or not yet bound, the condition silently fails and falls back to standard pagination, creating an inconsistent security model. More critically, the feature can be enabled via GET parameter without cryptographic verification, making it susceptible to privilege escalation through parameter manipulation in certain proxy/cache scenarios.",
      "suggestion": "Validate that organization_context.member is not None before accessing has_global_access. Consider using a signed/HMAC token or moving this authorization check into the paginator class itself to ensure it cannot be bypassed."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Data Access - Bounds Violation",
      "file": "src/sentry/api/paginator.py",
      "line": 869,
      "title": "Negative offset pagination bypasses queryset slice boundary validation",
      "description": "Lines 869-870 allow `cursor.offset` to remain negative when `enable_advanced_features=True`, passing it directly to Django queryset slicing (`queryset[start_offset:stop]`). While Django's ORM handles negative indices for lists, this is applied to QuerySet objects which do not safely support negative offsets—they silently evaluate to empty results or undefined behavior depending on the backend. This allows an attacker with admin rights to craft cursor values that leak data beyond intended pagination boundaries or cause resource exhaustion through inefficient query plans.",
      "suggestion": "Remove the special case for negative offsets at line 869. Always enforce `start_offset = max(0, offset)` regardless of `enable_advanced_features`, or implement explicit validation that offset >= 0 before passing to the queryset."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Logic Error - Incomplete Feature Design",
      "file": "src/sentry/api/paginator.py",
      "line": 843,
      "title": "OptimizedCursorPaginator does not override critical parent methods",
      "description": "OptimizedCursorPaginator inherits from BasePaginator but does not override `build_queryset()` or `count_hits()`. These methods likely depend on `self.key` and ordering configuration set by the parent. Without explicit implementation or clear inheritance semantics, the paginator may apply incorrect filtering or ordering when `enable_advanced_features=True`, leading to data consistency issues or silent query failures.",
      "suggestion": "Explicitly override `build_queryset()` in OptimizedCursorPaginator with documented behavior, or add assertions in `__init__` to verify required parent configuration is present. Document inherited behavior clearly."
    }
  ]
}
